title: cassinia laevis subsp. laevis
intro: Cassinia laevis subsp. laevis, also known as the New Zealand cassine or cassine, is a shrub-like plant native to New Zealand and parts of Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and dermatitis, as well as for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and stems can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are believed to offer various health benefits, including soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. Furthermore, Cassinia laevis subsp. laevis has been used in traditional medicine to aid in wound healing and to support the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
